articles.title: Come see my show LIVE in Washington DC June 13!! Spcial guests announced soon 👀#shorts #ufc #mma
Islam vs Ilia Is the Real Fight #ufc
articles.description: Here’s the problem Islam Makhachev is dealing with right now: People don’t care about anybody else. Not because Islam isn’t great — he is. He’s the world champion for a reason. Elite skill set. Dominant style. Proven winner. But there are only two fights fans truly want: Ilia Topuria and Arman Tsarukyan. That’s it. And the criticism surrounding Islam is growing because many fans believe he avoided both matchups when the opportunities were there. Now, fair or unfair, once that narrative starts, every other fight feels smaller. If Islam goes out and beats Ian Machado Garry tomorrow, are people suddenly going to rank it above beating Ilia or Arman? No chance. Because champions get judged differently. When you’re the best in the world, fans expect you to face the most dangerous threats available — especially the ones everyone is demanding. That’s why this conversation keeps coming back. Not because Islam isn’t respected. Not because he isn’t accomplished. But because there are two names attached to his legacy now. And until those fights happen, people are going to keep asking the same question: Why not? That’s the pressure that comes with being champion. And whether the criticism is deserved or not, the expectation isn’t going away. #UFC #MMA #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ArmanTsarukyan #IanGarry #FightTalk #MMADebate #CombatSports #UFCTitlePicture

articles.title: Ronda Says She’d “Clean Holly’s Clock”
articles.description: Ronda Rousey is retired. She already said she was only coming back for one fight with Gina Carano. And yet somehow, she's still dominating the conversation. After saying she would "clean Holly Holm's clock" in a rematch, Ronda once again found herself at the center of MMA headlines. But according to Chael Sonnen, that's exactly the point. Chael breaks down why the phrase was so effective. It wasn't disrespectful. It wasn't vulgar. It wasn't even particularly aggressive. But it accomplished exactly what every great promoter hopes to accomplish: it got people talking. Chael also examines Ronda's claim that beating Holly would "rewrite UFC history," a statement he believes was carefully crafted to provoke debate without crossing the line into trash talk. Whether Ronda fully believes everything she's saying or is simply playing the game better than everyone else, Chael sees a pattern emerging. Once again, Ronda Rousey found a way to stay relevant, stay in the headlines, and keep the conversation centered around her. And that's why Chael calls it another win. #RondaRousey #HollyHolm #ChaelSonnen #UFC #MMA #GinaCarano 0:00 Ronda’s Holly Holm Comment 0:24 Why “Clean Her Clock” Works 0:52 The Most Likeable Version of Ronda Yet 1:14 Is Ronda Serious or Performing? 1:46 What She Did Right During Gina Carano Build-Up 2:17 Ronda’s Pride Won’t Let Holly Go 2:47 Why the Phrase Matters 3:11 The “Rewrite UFC History” Claim 3:42 Why That Statement Provokes Debate 4:00 You Can’t Rewrite History Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Dana White Confirmed Chimaev’s Plan
articles.description: Dana White just confirmed what many suspected: Khamzat Chimaev is actively pushing for a title shot against Sean Strickland. And according to Chael Sonnen, that matters more than most people realize. Chael explains why the real battle isn't happening in the Octagon right now — it's happening in interviews, headlines, social media, and public perception. While Chimaev is publicly campaigning for the fight, Nassourdine Imavov remains largely silent despite having a legitimate argument for being next in line. Chael breaks down how title fights are actually secured, why fan interest matters, and why Sean Strickland may have already handed Imavov a huge advantage by publicly endorsing him as the rightful next challenger. But endorsements only go so far. From media strategy to public demand, Chael argues that Chimaev and Imavov are currently competing for the same opportunity — and only one of them appears to be actively fighting for it. This isn't a fight for rankings. It's a fight for attention. #UFC #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #NassourdineImavov #ChaelSonnen #MMA #DanaWhite 0:00 Dana White Confirms Chimaev's Push 0:33 The Only Other Contender Is Imavov 0:58 Why Chael Hasn't Heard From Imavov 1:39 What Chael Did When He Wanted a Title Shot 2:12 Chimaev's Team Is Working the Media 2:49 Chimaev vs Imavov Is Already Happening 3:03 Sean Strickland's Endorsement Matters 3:24 The Forgotten Strickland-Imavov Fight 3:52 A Fight for Clicks and Attention 4:29 Why Chimaev Has the Momentum 4:48 Imavov Has to Speak Up 5:05 The Rule Nobody Can Ignore Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: What Happened to Bob Menery? #ufc
articles.description: I was genuinely caught off guard watching this. Bob Menery is a guy I’ve watched for years. Fighters go on his show constantly. Big personality, always joking around, always asking fighters the same question: “How fast could you beat me in a street fight?” And every fighter laughs it off. Even Logan Paul had to straighten him out one time. But walking to the ring this time? Bob didn’t look like himself. He looked extremely thin. Jittery. Off. The kind of energy where you instantly notice something isn’t right. I’m not accusing him of anything — not drugs, not alcohol, none of that. I’m saying visually, from my own experience being around that environment growing up, he looked wired up. Like somebody running on stimulants and adrenaline at the same time. And then he gets in there with Johnny Manziel. People forget: Manziel is a real athlete. Heisman Trophy winner. First-round NFL talent. Explosive movement, instincts, timing — that stuff doesn’t just disappear. And once the fight started? It looked less like a celebrity match and more like a street fight with an actual athlete. Ugly. Fast. Chaotic. That’s what made the whole thing so uncomfortable to watch. Because the energy coming in already felt strange — and the fight only amplified it. #BobMenery #JohnnyManziel #LoganPaul #Boxing #CelebrityBoxing #FightNews #CombatSports #MMA #SportsDrama #FightTalk

articles.title: Joe Rogan Raised a Strange Point!
articles.description: Joe Rogan has always argued that combat sports are ultimately about discovering what works in a real fight. And that's exactly why Chael Sonnen was surprised by one of Rogan's recent comments about the proposed UFC event at the White House. According to Chael, if the entire purpose of mixed martial arts is to test fighting under realistic conditions, then why would fighting outdoors be viewed as a negative? From the early days of the UFC and the rise of Royce Gracie to the evolution of wrestling, boxing, Muay Thai, and takedown defense, Chael revisits the original idea behind mixed martial arts: finding out what actually works when the rules are stripped away. He also reacts to Ilia Topuria's fascinating comments about visualization and preparation, explaining why uncertainty itself may be one of the most realistic elements fighters can face. If weather, environment, and unexpected conditions are all part of real-world conflict, maybe the White House setting is closer to the spirit of MMA than many people realize. #UFC #JoeRogan #IliaTopuria #ChaelSonnen #MMA #WhiteHouseUFC 0:00 Joe Rogan’s Longtime Philosophy 0:36 What Works in a Real Fight? 1:20 Why Chael Agrees With Joe 1:55 The Royce Gracie Problem 2:10 Joe’s White House Comment 2:43 Why Does “Outside” Matter? 3:15 Fighting in the Rain3:36 Real Fights Don’t Have Perfect Conditions 3:50 Ilia Topuria’s Fascinating Observation 4:25 Why Uncertainty Matters 4:50 Chael’s Problem With the Criticism Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor McGregor’s Welterweight Advantage Is a Myth
articles.description: Is fighting at welterweight a competitive advantage for Conor McGregor? Chael Sonnen says absolutely not. After hearing the growing argument that Conor benefits from fighting at 170 pounds instead of cutting to lightweight, Chael explains why the logic doesn't hold up. In his view, making weight isn't a disadvantage — it's proof of discipline, focus, conditioning, and hard work. Drawing from wrestling, the Olympics, and his own experience, Chael argues that athletes only work as hard as they have to. The harder the weight cut, the more discipline and preparation are required. Remove that challenge, and you may remove some of what made the athlete dangerous in the first place. Chael also revisits comments Conor previously made about weight cutting, discusses why fighters often perform best when they force themselves into elite shape, and shares the story of a wrestler who became nearly unbeatable only after getting down to his competition weight. The question isn't whether Conor can fight at 170. The question is why anyone thinks it helps him. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCNews 0:00 Is 170 a Competitive Advantage for Conor? 0:16 Chael Strongly Disagrees 0:45 Athletes Only Work as Hard as They Have To 1:22 Why Making Weight Matters 1:55 155 vs 170 — Which Is Harder? 2:27 Conor’s View on Weight Cutting 3:00 Why Welterweight Isn't an Advantage 3:36 Weight Classes Aren't What Fans Think 4:06 The Josh Hokit Example 4:34 Doug Lee's Secret Weapon 5:04 Why Weight Cuts Create Killers 5:18 What's the Evidence? 5:30 "You Sound a Little Stupid" Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Why Colby Covington Belongs in the Hall of Fame
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reflects on the career of Colby Covington and explains why the Hall of Fame debate shouldn't even be a debate. After Colby's apparent retirement, Chael was surprised by the overwhelming response from fans defending his legacy. And the more he looks back at Colby's career, the stranger the story becomes. An interim champion who never lost his belt in competition. A clear No. 1 contender who waited years for a title shot. A fighter who defeated a reigning BMF champion but never became BMF champion himself. Chael breaks down how Colby changed the game outside the cage as well, becoming one of the first fighters to take promotion into his own hands by hiring his own camera crews and creating content when the UFC wasn't putting a spotlight on him. From Demian Maia and Robbie Lawler to Tyron Woodley, Colby's résumé includes wins over some of the biggest names of his generation. And while fans often focused on the character, Chael believes many are only now starting to appreciate the career. Sometimes you don't realize what you had until it's gone. #UFC #ColbyCovington #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCHallOfFame #RealAmericanFreestyle 0:00 Thank You for the Messages About Colby0:17 Is Colby Hall of Fame Worthy?0:52 Why This Question Matters1:25 You'll Miss Him When He's Gone2:00 Colby Took Promotion Into His Own Hands2:34 The First Fighter to Hire His Own Crew3:05 Colby's Career Sounds Like a Riddle3:17 The Interim Champion Who Never Lost3:47 The No. 1 Contender Who Waited Years4:02 The BMF Champion Who Never Got the Belt4:22 The Wins Everyone Forgets4:58 How Close Colby Was to Multiple Titles5:25 Chael's Message to Colby Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “Your Best Punch!” GSP Teaches Superman Punch To DJ
The UFC Doesn’t Want Islam vs Topuria
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to Ilia Topuria being asked once again about Islam Makhachev — and says Ilia handled the entire situation perfectly. According to Chael, Topuria didn’t manufacture drama, didn’t accuse Islam of ducking him, and didn’t force a fake rivalry. Instead, Ilia calmly explained that he believes the UFC itself may not want the fight to happen right now. Chael breaks down why Islam vs Topuria is such a rare matchup: two simultaneous double champions, both near the top of the pound-for-pound list, both still active, and both wanting the fight. But Chael also believes the conversation has gone too far. While everyone keeps asking Ilia about Islam, Arman Tsarukyan, and even Paddy Pimblett, nobody is talking about the man directly in front of him — Justin Gaethje. From interim titles and BMF belts to the UFC’s changing rules around simultaneous champions, Chael explains why the lightweight division may be much more complicated than fans realize. This isn’t just about Islam vs Topuria. It’s about timing, matchmaking, and who really deserves the next shot. #UFC #IliaTopuria #IslamMakhachev #JustinGaethje #ChaelSonnen #ArmanTsarukyan #MMA 0:00 Ilia Topuria Got Asked About Islam 0:39 Ilia Refused to Create Fake Drama 1:02 Two Double Champs Fighting?! 1:35 Why Chael Felt Bad for Ilia 2:16 The Race to Triple-Champ Status 2:47 Why Nobody Talks About Gaethje 3:44 Justin Gaethje’s Resume Is Insane 4:32 Why Tsarukyan Matters More 5:10 The UFC’s New Double-Champ Rules 6:00 Let Ilia Focus on Gaethje 6:52 Why Ilia Is So Valuable to Islam 7:24 The Other Dangerous Matchups for Islam 8:11 Ian Garry, Prates, and Morales 9:19 Why Nobody Demands Anything Anymore 10:08 Islam vs Ilia Is Way Down the Road Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Topuria Says He WON’T Wrestle Gaethje At UFC Freedom 250 | BISPING Reacts
articles.description: Michael Bisping reacts to Ilia Topuria saying he has no plans to wrestle Justin Gaethje and wants to keep their UFC 250 title fight as a pure striking battle. Bisping breaks down Topuria’s knockout power, his grappling background, Gaethje’s elite wrestling credentials, and why both men choosing to stand and trade could make this one of the most violent lightweight title fights imaginable. With UFC 250 heading to the White House event, Bisping also discusses the huge stakes, the atmosphere, and whether Topuria can extend his undefeated run against one of the most dangerous fighters in the sport. Who wins — Ilia Topuria or Justin Gaethje? Let us know in the comments. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join 🤖 Join The Discord: https://discord.gg/fbb9pc59 ▶️ Follow Michael on Social: https://www.instagram.com/mikebisping https://www.twitter.com/bisping https://www.facebook.com/mikethecountbisping ▶️ Edited by John Brannigan: https://www.instagram.com/johnbranniganuk https://www.twitter.com/johnbranniganuk #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #iliatopuria #justingaethje

articles.title: Paddy Pimblett’s Weights 191 lbs
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to Paddy Pimblett revealing that he currently weighs 191 pounds just six weeks before his next fight — and says the real conversation isn’t whether Paddy can make 155. It’s what happens long term. Chael breaks down why weight cutting remains one of the most misunderstood parts of combat sports, including the difference between cutting weight, being in shape, and using the scale as a built-in excuse after a loss. From Conor McGregor’s move up in weight to stories about college wrestling legend Doug Lee, Chael explains why some athletes actually need brutal weight cuts to force themselves into elite condition — and why moving up a division isn’t always the solution fans think it is. But Chael also warns that repeatedly cutting from nearly 190 pounds down to lightweight comes with a cost, and says the scale itself is what ultimately retires most fighters. This isn’t just about Paddy Pimblett’s number on the scale. It’s about discipline, performance, and the hidden toll of fight preparation. #UFC #PaddyPimblett #PaddyTheBaddy #ChaelSonnen #MMA #ConorMcGregor #UFCFightNight 0:00 Paddy Pimblett Weighs 191 Pounds 0:35 Why Fans Love Weight Stories 1:01 Why Did Paddy Reveal This? 1:45 Is Paddy Setting Up an Excuse? 2:29 Fighters and Weight-Cut Narratives 3:00 What Does Paddy Want From This? 3:38 Paddy Isn’t a Kid Anymore 4:08 Should Paddy Move to 170? 4:49 Why Cutting Weight Keeps Fighters Sharp 5:50 Chael’s Heavyweight Story 6:10 The Doug Lee Example 7:00 Why Extreme Cuts Shorten Careers 7:51 “The Scale Retires Fighters” 8:35 Does Paddy Only Train During Camp? 9:04 The Argument Against That Theory 9:31 Nobody Gets to Use Weight as an Excuse Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Rico vs Usyk Exposed Boxing #ufc
articles.description: Rico Verhoeven vs. Oleksandr Usyk has reignited one of the biggest debates in combat sports: how legitimate is modern boxing compared to MMA and kickboxing? According to this breakdown, Rico went out there and physically handled Usyk in ways many fans never expected. And when you combine that performance with recent crossover fights involving Conor McGregor and Francis Ngannou, people are beginning to ask serious questions about boxing’s credibility. McGregor lasted deep into a fight with Floyd Mayweather Jr. despite having zero professional boxing experience. Ngannou dropped Tyson Fury and many fans — including one official judge — believed Francis deserved the win. Now Rico enters the conversation. That’s what makes this situation so fascinating. Every time a fighter from another combat sport steps into boxing and immediately succeeds, the mythology around boxing superiority gets challenged. And in this case, the argument becomes even stronger because the fight went the distance. Once a fight officially reaches the scorecards, hypothetical future rounds no longer matter. So now the question shifts directly to Usyk himself: was that really the right outcome? Subscribe for more combat sports analysis, crossover fight breakdowns, and Chael-style commentary. #RicoVerhoeven #OleksandrUsyk #Boxing #FrancisNgannou #ConorMcGregor #TysonFury #CombatSports

articles.title: Kamaru Usman vs Dricus Du Plessis Is Happening  #ufc
articles.description: Kamaru Usman vs. Dricus du Plessis could be one of the biggest shakeups the UFC middleweight division has seen in years. According to this breakdown, the fight is close to being announced — and while the matchup itself is incredible, the real intrigue is what it means for the title picture at 185 pounds. Du Plessis continues building a reputation for taking the toughest fights available. Meanwhile, Usman finally appears ready to move permanently to middleweight after years of fans calling for it. But if there’s no championship on the line, then why make this fight now? That’s the mystery. Both Usman and Du Plessis own massive wins over Sean Strickland, which immediately complicates the division. At the same time, questions still surround a possible rematch involving Khamzat Chimaev after the close battle with Strickland. So where does the winner go? A title shot? A number-one contender position? Or is the UFC quietly building something even bigger behind the scenes? One thing is clear: this isn’t a random fight. There’s a reason it’s happening, and the outcome could completely reshape the future of the middleweight division. Subscribe for more UFC analysis, fight breakdowns, and Chael-style MMA commentary. #KamaruUsman #DricusDuPlessis #UFC #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #MMA #Middleweight

articles.title: Khamzat Chimaev’s Excuse Is Working
articles.description: Chael Sonnen says the conversation around Khamzat Chimaev’s brutal weight cut completely missed the real story. Yes, the cut looked miserable. Yes, TJ Dillashaw explained how Chimaev reportedly failed to water load properly before the weigh-in. But according to Chael, none of that actually matters anymore — because Chimaev made weight. Instead, Chael believes the entire aftermath became something much more valuable: a built-in narrative for a rematch with Sean Strickland. Chael breaks down the science of water loading, how fighters manipulate hydration before weigh-ins, and why cutting weight and losing weight are two completely different things. But the deeper point is promotional. From Ronda Rousey’s ability to build fights through indirect conflict to the UFC’s middleweight title picture involving Nassourdine Imavov, Chael explains why Chimaev vs Strickland remains the only storyline people are talking about — even after the fight is over. This isn’t really about weight cutting anymore. It’s about attention, narratives, and how rematches get sold. #UFC #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #ChaelSonnen #MMA #Imavov #UFCMiddleweight 0:00 Chimaev’s Weight Cut Won’t Go Away 0:39 He Actually Made Weight 1:14 What Counts as Success? 1:45 TJ Dillashaw Explains Water Loading2 :49 Why Water Loading Works 3:24 Chimaev’s Body Wasn’t Ready 4:28 The Weight Cut Isn’t the Real Story 5:00 Chael’s Bigger Point About Promotion 5:42 Why Imavov Should Be Next 6:11 Strickland Already Gave Imavov His Blessing 6:40 Chimaev and Strickland Found a New Angle 7:00 The Built-In Excuse for the Rematch Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.description: In the NEWEST episode of the DC show with Daniel Cormier, we're joined by none other than sports media personality and former NFL superstar, Chad "Ochocinco" Johnson! Plus, Johnson talks about his illustrious NFL career as one of the top wide receivers in the sport with the Cincinnati Bengals, and why he has NO REGRETS over all the fines and touchdown celebrations looking back on it all. articles.title: Johnny Manziel did NOT hurt Bob Menery
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to the bizarre fight between Bob Menery and Johnny Manziel — and says the strangest part wasn’t the loss itself. Johnny Manziel may not be a trained fighter, but he’s still an elite athlete, and according to Chael, once the fight started it became obvious very quickly that Bob Menery was in over his head. But the real story began after the stoppage. Chael breaks down Bob’s post-fight comments, including his demand for a rematch and his insistence that he “wasn’t hurt” during the TKO. From there, Chael dives into a deeper point about what fighting actually is — and why many people who watch combat sports still fundamentally misunderstand what a stoppage means. Chael also reacts to Bob’s appearance walking to the ring, the strange energy around the event, and the difference between looking tough online and understanding what happens in a real fight. This isn’t just about Bob Menery vs Johnny Manziel. It’s about the reality of combat when the cameras stop rolling. #MMA #BobMenery #JohnnyManziel #ChaelSonnen #Boxing #CombatSports 0:00 Bob Menery Fought Johnny Manziel 0:34 Chael Was Shocked by Bob’s Appearance 1:15 “He Looked Like He Was Tweaking” 2:02 The Commission’s Awkward Position 2:25 Johnny Manziel Beats Him Fast 3:02 The Story Actually Starts After the Fight 3:42 Bob Asked for a Rematch 4:30 “The Gig Is Up” 5:05 Bob’s Face Was Covered in Damage 5:48 “I Wasn’t Hurt” 6:24 The Part Chael Couldn’t Believe 7:05 Bob Doesn’t Understand Fighting 8:02 What “Hurt” Really Means in a Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Ngannou opens up about losing son, Kobe at just 15 months | The Daniel Cormier Show
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by former UFC heavyweight champion, and one of the biggest knockout artists in MMA history, Francis Ngannou. Francis opens up about his son, Kobe passing away at just 15 months old, and how he never wanted to fight again after the family tragedy. DC experienced the same tragedy as he lost his 3-month old daughter in a car accident, and the two talk about how this was the hardest thing they have had to deal with in their lives. Francis talks about finding the strength to continue fighting, and honoring his son every time he steps inside the cage. All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: We Got the Usyk vs Rico Finish WRONG
articles.description: Chael Sonnen is walking back his original reaction to the controversial finish in the Usyk vs Rico heavyweight title fight. After rewatching the entire fight from start to finish, Chael says the story may not be corruption, incompetence, or a robbery at all. Instead, he breaks down why the referee may have been fully justified in stopping the fight — even with the bell seconds away. Chael explains how public reaction, replay clips, commentary, and social media narratives shaped the outrage around the stoppage, including Todd Grisham’s viral call during the finish. But once the full fight is viewed in context, the situation looks very different. From unanswered punches and referee discretion to boxing’s unwritten “three unanswered shots” rule, Chael breaks down the exact sequence that changed his mind. This isn’t just about Usyk and Rico. It’s about how fight fans — and even analysts — react before seeing the full picture. #Boxing #OleksandrUsyk #Usyk #HeavyweightBoxing #MMA #ChaelSonnen #Rico 0:00 I Take It All Back1:12 Todd Grisham Influenced Everyone2:23 The Conspiracy Theory Falls Apart3:03 Rico Was Getting Beaten Up3:53 The Bell Didn’t Save Him4:17 Chael’s Wrestling Comparison5:24 Nobody Actually Watched the Fight6:40 The Real Question About the Bell7:10 Rico Was Taking Unanswered Punches8:15 Why Standing TKOs Are Rare9:02 The Reward for Swarming a Fighter10:00 The Ref Had the Right to Stop It Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Worst Part About Rico vs Usyk.
articles.description: Rico Verhoeven may have done enough against Oleksandr Usyk… …but Chael Sonnen believes none of that matters now. Chael breaks down the brutal reality of crossover fights, boxing politics, and why Rico could be stuck in the worst position possible — taking the risk, proving himself on the biggest stage, and still walking away with nothing. He explains why Usyk deserves credit for surviving, why Rico may never get a rematch, and how the business side of boxing ultimately decides everything. Chael also dives into the strange production of the event itself — from the oversized walkouts and endless delays to the uncomfortable reality that promoters may not have believed the fight alone could sell. From Tyson Fury vs Francis Ngannou comparisons to the deeper problems with boxing presentation, this becomes less about Rico vs Usyk… and more about what the sport has turned into. #Boxing #OleksandrUsyk #RicoVerhoeven #TysonFury #FrancisNgannou #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Why Rico’s position is devastating 0:43 The impossible challenge Rico accepted 1:07 What Usyk believed during the fight 1:47 Usyk thought he needed a finish 2:35 Why Usyk still deserves credit 3:18 The rematch clause problem 4:02 Why Rico probably won’t get another fight 5:04 Why the first fight failed commercially 6:30 Fury’s conversation with the referee 7:30 “This is all make-believe” 7:52 Jake Paul’s criticism of boxing events 8:16 Why the production was bigger than the fight 9:14 Boxing promoters don’t trust the product 9:35 Chael compares boxing to pro wrestling 10:04 The real issue with boxing today 10:31 When production outshines the sport Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
